mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in 65% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and margin compression, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in 70% of industrial production contraction periods within late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates surpassing 4.0 percent trigger immediate liquidity withdrawal and higher borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in 60% of cases or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a post-recession recovery phase drives earnings revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth accelerates within recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal improving supply-side momentum that immediately boosts equity risk appetite, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window or 5-day forward returns are negative in 58% of expansion periods.
